text:
Admission Date:  1990-06-15              Discharge Date:   2009-06-24

Date of Birth:  2013-01-16             Sex:   M

Service: NEUROSURGERY

Allergies:
Heparin Agents

Attending:Taylor
Chief Complaint:
Profound weakness in upper extremities; Central cord syndrome

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Posterior cervical decompression laminectomy C3-6 on 1976-06-06 by
Dr. Mary Rodriguez


History of Present Illness:
Pt is a 57 year old male who was transferred to Saint Joseph - Martin on 07-24
from Morehouse General Hospital Medical Center after sustaining a cervical spinal cord
injury on 2011-11-15.  The pt was reported to be in shallow water
when he was hit by a wave and fell hitting his neck. He
immediately was not able to move his arms or legs. He was
rescued from the water and brought to the local hospital where
IV steroids and vasopressor therapy were instituted. Sensation
and movement began to return to his lower extremities and he
slowly developed some return of strength into his distal upper
extremities. Paresthesia are present in all extremities. There
has been associated autonomic dysfunction. He has had problems
with ileus formation, orthostatic hypotension requiring Florinef
therapy, and paroxysmal sweating. After pt stabilized he was air
transferred to Saint Joseph - Martin to be treated closer to his family and
support systems.

Past Medical History:
Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia

Social History:
Lives in [**State 1727**], employed as a Professor. His daughter is a
medical intern at Saint Joseph - Martin. No tobacco use. Rare ETOH use.

Family History:
Non contributory

Physical Exam:
On Admission: In NAD. Awake, alert, attentive. Good historian.
Speech is fluent with no phonetic or semantic paraphrasing.
Stream of thoughts is fluid. Toungue is midline without
fasciculations.  PERRLA, 4mm to 2mm with light. EOM's full. No
nystagmus. Facial sensation intact, facial strength full. Motor:
 Normal bulk. Normal tone. No pronator drift.

Posterior cervical incision is well approximated with patent
surgical staples which will be due for removal in 07-05 days
following surgery.There is no drainage, edema, or erythema
noted.

STANDING CERVICAL XRAYS:
CERVICAL SPINE, AP, LATERAL AND SWIMMER'S VIEWS: Patient is
status post
posterior cervical fusion with two spinal rods and pedicle
screws at C2 and
C4-C7 on the left and C2-C7 on the right. Overall alignment is
similar to the
prior study. As before there are extensive degenerative changes
at multiple
levels predominating in the lower cervical spine including disc
space
narrowing, endplate irregularities and marginal osteophyte
formation. There
are skin staples along the posterior neck. Mild prevertebral
soft tissue
swelling persists.
IMPRESSION: Status post cervical fusion from C2-C7 without
evidence of
hardware loosening or other complication.

DROP IN PLATELET COUNT SUSPICIOUS FOR HIT, HIT ANTIBODY SENT,
RESULTS *****POSITIVE*****


Brief Hospital Course:
On 1976-06-06 the patient went to the O.R. for posterior cervical
decompression laminectomy C3-7 with C2-7 fusion by Dr. Mary
Rodriguez. There were no perioperative complications to report.
PT/OT and Autonomic Neurology have been consulted and
recommendations instituted. When OOB he wears an abdominal
binder and his bowel function has improved. The patient had
difficulty with voiding so his foley was reinserted 1974-01-28. On
09-04 a drop in platlets was noted and his SC Hep was
discontinued and HIT anitbody sent to lab. It was grossly
positive and Argatroban gtt was started along with Hematology
consult. His Platlets began to trend up and he was cleared to go
to rehab. The patient's strength has been improving. He
continues to maintain a positive outlook and is fully
participatory in his post surgical care. His staples were
removed and his incision is C/D/IHe is eager to begin the next
phase of his rehabilitation. The patient will be discharged to
rehab.

Medications on Admission:
Florinef, Colace, Effexor, Protonix, Lovenox

Discharge Medications:
1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
2. Venlafaxine 25 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO TID (3 times
a day).
3. Simvastatin 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
4. Miconazole Nitrate 2 % Powder Sig: One (1) Appl Topical TID
(3 times a day) as needed for armpit rash.
5. Acetaminophen 325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO Q6H (every 6
hours) as needed.
6. Fludrocortisone 0.1 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).

Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily): 03 give
suppository or PO.
10. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2
times a day) for 8 days.
11. Tamsulosin 0.4 mg Capsule, Sust. Release 24 hr Sig: One (1)
Capsule, Sust. Release 24 hr PO 30 min after PM meal.
12. Tramadol 50 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q4H (every 4
hours) as needed.
13. Metoclopramide 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO QIDACHS
(4 times a day (before meals and at bedtime)).
14. Argatroban 100 mg/mL Solution Sig: 0.5-2 mcg/kg/min
Intravenous INFUSION (continuous infusion): Please titrate to
aPTT 60-80secs

**Currently at 2.5mcg/kg for 24hr***.


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
K Hovnanian Childrens Hospital Rehab Center at Baylor Medical Center At Trophy Club Center

Discharge Diagnosis:
Central cord Syndrome
Urinary retention
Heparin Induced Thrombocytopenia


Discharge Condition:
Stable


Discharge Instructions:
??????	Do not smoke.
??????	Keep your wound clean / No tub baths or pool swimming until
seen in follow up.
??????	No pulling up, lifting more than 10 lbs., or excessive bending
or twisting.
??????	Limit your use of stairs to 2-3 times per day.
??????	Have your incision checked daily for signs of infection.
??????	Take your pain medication as instructed; you may find it best
if taken in the morning when you wake-up for morning stiffness,
and before bed for sleeping discomfort.
??????	Do not take any anti-inflammatory medications such as Motrin,
Advil, Aspirin, and Ibuprofen etc. for 3 months.
??????	Increase your intake of fluids and fiber, as pain medicine
(narcotics) can cause constipation. We recommend taking an over
the counter stool softener, such as Docusate (Colace) while
taking narcotic pain medication.

CALL YOUR SURGEON IMMEDIATELY IF YOU EXPERIENCE ANY OF THE
FOLLOWING:

??????	Pain that is continually increasing or not relieved by pain
medicine.
??????	Any weakness, numbness, tingling in your extremities.
??????	Any signs of infection at the wound site: redness, swelling,
tenderness, and drainage.
??????	Fever greater than or equal to 101?????? F.
??????	Any change in your bowel or bladder habits (such as loss of
bowl or urine control).
. You will be going to rehab with your foley catheter, you will
follow up with your urologist for further follow up and
treatment.

Followup Instructions:
Dr. Morris will also need to see you in 6 weeks. Please call for
an appointment. 323-651-4088. You will also need xrays at this
appt.

Follow up with Urology ( pt. has a pre-existing appointment with
Dr. Johnny Lewis on 03-14).
